title: Create and provision companies
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to create and provision new tenant companies on the platform, so that subscribing organizations can begin using the HRMS.
acceptance_criteria: Given I hold the HRMS Administrator role, when I initiate creation of a new company, then a new tenant company record is created and provisioned on the platform.; Given a company is being provisioned, when provisioning runs, then a company-specific isolated data space is established for its organizational structures, policies, security, workflows, and workforce data.; Given a company is provisioned, when provisioning completes, then the company becomes available for further configuration and onboarding.; Given I do not hold the HRMS Administrator role, when I attempt to create a company, then the action is denied.

title: Maintain business and legal details
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to record and maintain my company's business and legal details, so that the platform holds accurate identifying, legal, and contact information.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company record, when I edit its profile, then I can capture legal name, trade name, and website.; Given a company record, when I edit its profile, then I can capture registration identifiers including GSTN and EIN.; Given a company record, when I edit its profile, then I can capture contact information.; Given required business and legal fields are provided, when I save, then the details persist against the company; Given a field is missing or malformed, when I save, then a validation error is shown and the record is not saved.; Given business or legal details are updated later, when I save changes, then the updated values are retained against the company.

title: Operate across multiple jurisdictions
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want my company to operate across one or more jurisdictions, so that we can run operations wherever we do business under a single tenant.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when I configure jurisdictions, then the company can be associated with one or more jurisdictions.; Given multiple jurisdictions are configured, when the company operates, then each jurisdiction is supported without requiring a separate tenant.; Given a company operates in a jurisdiction, when I configure it, then jurisdiction-specific organizational structures, policies, security, workflows, and workforce data can be applied per jurisdiction.

title: Company-specific configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want company-specific configuration of organizational structures, policies, security, workflows, and workforce data, so that our setup reflects our own operating model.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when I configure it, then organizational structures, policies, security settings, workflows, and workforce data can each be set specifically for that company.; Given company-specific configuration exists, when another company is configured, then its settings remain independent and do not affect mine.; Given I am scoped to my company, when I change configuration, then the change applies only within my company's isolated data space.

title: Company-level data isolation
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the system to preserve data isolation at the company level, so that each tenant's data remains private and secure from other tenants.
acceptance_criteria: Given multiple companies exist on the platform, when data is stored or accessed, then each company's data is isolated from every other company.; Given a user is scoped to one company, when they access data, then they cannot access another company's data.; Given a company operates as standalone, within a group, or under a shared services provider, when data is accessed, then company-level isolation is enforced regardless of the operating model.

title: Support standalone, group, and shared-services operating models
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to support companies operating as standalone, within a group-company structure, or under an external shared services provider, so that the platform fits diverse ownership and management arrangements.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when I set its operating model, then it can be designated as an independent standalone company.; Given a company, when I set its operating model, then it can be designated as part of a group-company structure.; Given a company, when I set its operating model, then it can be designated as managed by an external shared services provider.; Given any of these models is selected, when data is accessed, then company-level data isolation is still preserved.; Given an operating model is set, when it changes later, then isolation and configuration continue to apply correctly under the new model.

title: Commercial packaging and subscription models
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want commercial packaging and subscription models based on number of companies, number of employees, and subscribed modules, so that customers can be billed according to their usage and entitlements.
acceptance_criteria: Given a subscription, when it is configured, then packaging can be based on number of companies.; Given a subscription, when it is configured, then packaging can be based on number of employees.; Given a subscription, when it is configured, then packaging can be based on the set of subscribed modules.; Given a subscription is defined, when a customer subscribes, then the applicable package and limits are applied.; Given multiple packaging dimensions are combined, when the subscription is priced, then company count, employee count, and module selection are all reflected.

title: Company inactivation and data retention on closure
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to mark companies inactive upon closure and retain their data per policy, so that closed or merged companies are handled compliantly.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company closes, when I process the closure, then the company is marked inactive.; Given a company is closed or merged, when it is archived, then its data is preserved for 7 years subject to archival fees.; Given a company is inactive, when the retention period applies, then data retention follows the defined policy.; Given a company is marked inactive, when users attempt normal operations, then active operations are disabled while retained data remains available for retention purposes.

title: Export entire company data on platform exit
user_story: As a Company Admin of a customer leaving the platform, I want to export the entire company data, so that we retain our records when we discontinue the service.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer is leaving the platform, when they request an export, then the entire company data can be exported.; Given an export is generated, when it completes, then it contains the company's full dataset for handover.; Given the export is scoped to my company, when it is produced, then it includes only my company's data and no other tenant's data.

title: Manage companies within a group-company structure
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to manage the set of companies that belong to my group-company structure, so that related entities are administered together while remaining isolated.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group-company structure exists, when I view the group, then I can see and administer the companies that belong to the group.; Given a company is part of a group, when I configure it, then group-level oversight is possible while each member company's data remains isolated at the company level.; Given I am a Group Company Admin, when I attempt to access a company outside my group, then the action is denied.

title: Manage companies as an external shared services provider
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin acting as an external shared services provider, I want to manage the companies assigned to me, so that I can administer multiple client tenants on their behalf.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company is designated as managed by an external shared services provider, when I am assigned as its administrator, then I can administer that company's configuration and data.; Given I manage multiple client companies, when I access each one, then company-level data isolation is preserved between clients.; Given I am not assigned to a company, when I attempt to access it, then the action is denied.

title: Enforce subscription entitlements and limits
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the platform to enforce the limits of each subscription package, so that usage stays within the customer's entitlements for companies, employees, and modules.
acceptance_criteria: Given a subscription defines a company limit, when the limit would be exceeded, then further company creation is prevented or flagged.; Given a subscription defines an employee limit, when the limit would be exceeded, then the excess is prevented or flagged per policy.; Given a subscription defines a set of subscribed modules, when a user accesses a module, then access is granted only for subscribed modules.; Given entitlements change, when the subscription is updated, then the applied limits and module access are updated accordingly.

title: Apply archival fees for retained closed-company data
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to apply archival fees for the data of closed or merged companies retained for 7 years, so that long-term retention is billed according to policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given a closed or merged company's data is archived, when it is retained, then it is preserved for 7 years subject to archival fees.; Given archival fees apply, when the retention period is active, then the fees are tracked and billable to the customer.; Given the 7-year retention period elapses, when retention ends, then the data is handled per the defined disposal or retention policy.

title: Preserve and access data for closed or merged companies
user_story: As a Company Admin of a closed or merged company, I want our data preserved and accessible per the retention policy, so that we can meet compliance and record-keeping obligations after closure.
acceptance_criteria: Given my company is marked inactive on closure, when the retention policy applies, then our data is preserved for 7 years.; Given my company was merged into another, when data is archived, then the merged company's records remain preserved per policy.; Given retained data exists, when I need our records, then I can request an export of the entire company data for handover.

title: Enforce uniqueness and integrity of company registration identifiers
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want registration identifiers and legal identity validated for uniqueness and integrity at the data layer, so that no two companies collide on regulated identifiers and records stay trustworthy.
acceptance_criteria: Given company registration identifiers such as GSTN and EIN, when a company record is created or updated, then the platform enforces uniqueness and format-integrity constraints on those identifiers at the data layer.; Given a duplicate GSTN or EIN, when I attempt to save, then the record is rejected with an integrity error.; Given legal name and trade name, when a record is persisted, then not-null and referential integrity constraints are enforced so the canonical company entity remains valid.

title: Maintain bitemporal history of company profile and lifecycle status
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want changes to my company's profile and lifecycle status to be effective-dated and historically preserved, so that we can see what was true at any point in time.
acceptance_criteria: Given a change to business, legal, or contact details, when I save it with an effective date, then the prior values are retained as history and the new values apply from the effective date.; Given a lifecycle status change such as active, inactive, or merged, when it is recorded, then the change is effective-dated and previous states remain queryable.; Given a point in time, when I query the company record, then the platform returns the values that were effective and recorded as of that time.

title: Model group-company relationships in the data model
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want the parent-subsidiary relationships between companies in my group represented in the canonical data model, so that group structure is queryable while each company stays isolated.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies belonging to a group, when the group structure is defined, then parent-subsidiary relationships are stored as first-class relationships in the data model.; Given a group relationship, when it is persisted, then referential integrity links member companies to the group without collapsing their company-level data isolation.; Given the group changes over time, when membership is added or removed, then the relationship changes are effective-dated and preserved.

title: Toggle subscribed modules via governed configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want subscribed modules represented as per-tenant governed configuration, so that enabling or disabling modules changes system behavior without code.
acceptance_criteria: Given my company's subscription, when subscribed modules are set, then each module toggle is stored as versioned, effective-dated per-tenant configuration.; Given a module toggle changes, when it is saved, then the change is applied without a code deployment and the prior configuration version is retained.; Given the module configuration, when the engines read it at runtime, then only toggled-on modules are treated as enabled for my company.

title: Define subscription packages as governed versioned config
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want subscription packages and their limits defined as governed configuration, so that packaging by companies, employees, and modules can be changed and versioned without code.
acceptance_criteria: Given commercial packaging, when I define a subscription package, then its company-count, employee-count, and module-set limits are stored as versioned, effective-dated configuration.; Given a package definition changes, when I save a new version, then the change is effective-dated and prior versions remain auditable.; Given a customer subscribes, when the package is applied, then the engines read the current package configuration to determine entitlements.

title: Rules engine evaluates subscription entitlements from config
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the shared Rules engine to evaluate subscription entitlements from configured package limits, so that entitlement decisions are consistent and driven by config rather than hard-coded logic.
acceptance_criteria: Given configured package limits, when a company or employee is created or a module is accessed, then the Rules engine evaluates the request against the package decision table.; Given the evaluation, when a limit would be exceeded, then the engine returns a deny or flag decision that the platform enforces.; Given package configuration changes, when the engine next evaluates, then it uses the updated limits without any code change.

title: Company directory with search filter and navigation
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a company directory screen with search, filter, and navigation, so that I can quickly find and open the companies I administer.
acceptance_criteria: Given companies I am entitled to see, when I open the company directory, then they are presented in a metadata-driven grid scoped to my access.; Given the directory, when I search or filter by attributes such as legal name, trade name, jurisdiction, or status, then the grid updates to matching companies only.; Given a company row, when I select it, then I navigate to that company's detail screen; Given I lack access to a company, when I search, then it does not appear in my results.

title: Metadata-driven company profile screen
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven company profile screen to view and edit business, legal, jurisdiction, and configuration details, so that I can self-serve company setup through the SPA.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when I open its profile screen, then business, legal, contact, jurisdiction, and configuration sections render from metadata-driven forms.; Given I edit a field, when validation rules from configuration apply, then inline validation feedback is shown before save.; Given I save valid changes, when the form submits via the API/BFF, then the changes persist and the screen reflects the updated values.; Given fields I am not permitted to edit, when the screen renders, then those fields are read-only or hidden per my role.

title: Guided six-step company creation wizard
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want a guided six-step wizard to create a new company, so that I can capture all required onboarding information in a structured flow.
acceptance_criteria: Given I start company creation, when the wizard opens, then it presents six steps in order: Step 1 Basic Information (legal name, trade name, website), Step 2 Legal Details (registration identifiers, incorporation date), Step 3 Contact Information (primary address, billing address), Step 4 Operational Configuration (jurisdictions, base currency, time zone), Step 5 Administrative Setup (initial company administrator), and Step 6 Review and Confirm.; Given I am in the wizard, when I progress, then a step indicator shows the current step (1-6).; Given I have incomplete data, when I choose Save as Draft, then the company is saved in Draft status and can be resumed later.; Given the Create Company action, when the wizard is available, then it is visible only to Platform Admin (Platform Super Administrator and Platform Operations Admin) roles.; Given I complete Step 6 and confirm, when I submit, then the company is created.

title: Validate mandatory fields on company creation
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want mandatory company fields validated before creation, so that no company is created with missing critical data.
acceptance_criteria: Given the creation wizard, when I attempt to proceed or submit, then the system validates that Legal Name, Primary Jurisdiction, Base Currency, Time Zone, and Primary Contact Email are all provided.; Given Legal Name is entered, when validated, then it must be unique across the platform or creation is blocked with COMP_001.; Given any mandatory field is missing, when I try to continue, then a validation error is shown and the step is not passed.; Given all mandatory fields are valid, when I submit, then the company record is created.

title: Support multiple registration identifier types
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to record my company's registration identifier using the correct type for my jurisdiction, so that legal identity is captured accurately.
acceptance_criteria: Given legal details, when I select a registration type, then GST Number (India), EIN (USA), Company Registration Number (UK), and a configurable Custom identifier type are all supported.; Given India jurisdiction and a GST Number, when I save, then the GST number must be 15 characters or a validation error is shown.; Given a Custom identifier type, when configured, then I can capture a jurisdiction-appropriate identifier.; Given a registration number, when saved, then it is stored against RegistrationType and RegistrationNumber.

title: Automatic tenant provisioning actions on company creation
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the platform to automatically perform setup actions when a company is created, so that a new tenant is ready to operate immediately.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company is created, when provisioning runs, then the system automatically creates the tenant database schema and initializes default security policies.; Given an initial administrator email is provided, when the company is created, then a default company administrator user is created and a welcome email is sent to that administrator.; Given a company is created, when the event completes, then a COMPANY_CREATED event is logged in the audit trail with the creator and company data.; Given no administrator email is provided, when the company is created, then tenant schema and default policies are still initialized.

title: Detect and warn on potential duplicate companies
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to be warned about potential duplicate companies during creation, so that I do not create redundant tenants.
acceptance_criteria: Given a legal name, when I enter it, then the system checks legal name uniqueness by exact match and rejects duplicates with COMP_001.; Given a registration identifier, when I enter it, then the system checks uniqueness within the jurisdiction and rejects duplicates with COMP_006.; Given potential non-exact matches exist, when I proceed, then a modal dialog displays the list of potential matching companies as a warning.; Given the duplicate warning modal, when I review it, then I can choose to cancel or continue creation.

title: Auto-generate human-readable company code
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want each company assigned a unique human-readable code, so that companies can be referenced consistently.
acceptance_criteria: Given a new company, when it is created, then the system generates a Company Code in the format COMP-{YYYY}-{NNNN} where YYYY is the year of creation and NNNN is a sequential number from 0001 to 9999.; Given the creation wizard, when I am on the Basic Information step, then a Company Code preview is displayed.; Given a company code, when the record is saved, then the code is unique and non-editable.; Given the sequential number, when a new company is created in a given year, then NNNN increments for that year.

title: Enforce field-level validation rules on company data
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want company fields validated against defined rules and formats, so that stored data is well-formed and compliant.
acceptance_criteria: Given Legal Name, when I save, then it must be 3-100 characters or the error 'Legal name is required and must be 3-100 characters' is shown.; Given Website, when provided, then it must be a valid URL or the error 'Please enter a valid URL' is shown.; Given Primary Email, when I save, then it must be a valid email format or the error 'Please enter a valid email address' is shown.; Given GST Number and India jurisdiction, when I save, then it must be 15 characters or the error 'GST number must be 15 characters' is shown.; Given Base Currency, Time Zone, Language, and Primary Color, when saved, then they must conform to ISO 4217, IANA timezone, ISO 639-1, and hex #RRGGBB formats respectively.; Given Employee Limit, when saved, then it must be greater than 0 and Subscription Tier must be one of Basic, Standard, or Enterprise.

title: Enforce editability constraints on company master data
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want editing rules enforced on company fields, so that immutable data is protected while editable data can be maintained.
acceptance_criteria: Given Legal Name, when I edit it, then the change is allowed and an audit log entry is created.; Given Company Code, when I view the record, then it is non-editable as a system-generated value.; Given Jurisdictions, when I edit, then I can add jurisdictions but cannot remove a jurisdiction that has employees assigned.; Given Base Currency, when a first transaction has occurred, then it becomes non-editable.; Given an edit to a suspended or inactive company, when I attempt to modify it, then it is blocked (COMP_004 suspended / COMP_005 inactive) per lifecycle rules.

title: Company lifecycle state machine with approval workflow
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want company status transitions governed by a defined state machine and approvals, so that lifecycle changes are controlled and valid.
acceptance_criteria: Given company statuses, when a company progresses, then only these states exist: Draft, Active, Suspended, Inactive, Archived.; Given a Draft company, when setup completes, then it transitions to Active automatically, and Draft may alternatively be Cancelled.; Given an Active company, when transitioning to Suspended or to Inactive, then Platform Super Administrator approval is required (Inactive additionally requires confirmation).; Given a Suspended company, when transitioning back to Active, then Platform Super Administrator approval is required.; Given an Inactive company, when the 7-year retention period elapses, then it transitions to Archived automatically, and Archived is a terminal state with no further transitions.; Given an invalid transition, when attempted, then it is rejected.

title: Company suspension behavior and access control
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want defined behavior when a company is suspended, so that access is restricted while in-flight work is handled gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company is suspended, when users attempt to log in, then new logins are prevented for all except Platform Administrators.; Given existing sessions, when the company is suspended, then read-only access is allowed for a grace period of 30 minutes.; Given background jobs, when suspension occurs, then queued background jobs are allowed to complete.; Given suspension, when it is applied, then a notification is sent to the company administrators.; Given a suspended company, when a normal operation is attempted, then it is blocked with COMP_004.

title: Company inactivation (closure) processing
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want a defined inactivation process on company closure, so that accounts, workflows, and data are handled correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company is inactivated, when closure is processed, then all user accounts are disabled.; Given pending workflows, when inactivation runs, then they are completed or cancelled with an audit entry.; Given inactivation, when it completes, then a company data export package is generated and a 7-year data retention timer is initiated.; Given inactivation, when it completes, then a confirmation is sent to the company administrators and Platform Operations.; Given an inactive company, when modification is attempted, then it is blocked with COMP_005.

title: Company archival with verified export and secure deletion
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want company archival to produce a verified export and then securely delete data, so that the terminal Archived state is reached with data integrity assured.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company reaches archival, when the archive is produced, then it includes a complete data export in JSON/CSV format, an audit log export, and a document attachment export.; Given the export package, when generated, then a verification checksum is produced to confirm data integrity.; Given archival confirmation, when integrity is verified, then the data is securely deleted.; Given archival completes, when the status changes, then the company is set to Archived (terminal state).

title: Enforce data retention rules by data type
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want retention periods and post-retention actions enforced per data type, so that the platform stays compliant.
acceptance_criteria: Given employee records, when an employee exits, then records are retained 7 years then anonymized and archived.; Given transactional data, when retained, then it is kept 7 years then archived to cold storage.; Given audit logs, when retained, then they are kept 7 years then archived to compliance storage.; Given documents, when retained, then they are kept 7 years then exported and deleted.; Given backup data, when retained, then it is purged after 90 days.; Given a deleted company record, when soft-deleted, then it is purged 90 days after deletion.

title: Company-level configuration settings
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure authentication, security, localization, notification, and workflow defaults for my company, so that platform behavior matches our policies.
acceptance_criteria: Given authentication configuration, when I set allowed auth methods, then I can enable SAML, AD, O365, Google, and Local.; Given security configuration, when I set it, then I can configure MFA requirement, password policy, and session timeout.; Given localization configuration, when I set it, then I can configure date format, number format, and currency display.; Given notification configuration, when I set it, then I can configure default email templates and sender configuration.; Given workflow defaults, when I set them, then I can configure default approval hierarchies and SLA settings.; Given configuration is scoped to my company, when saved, then it applies only within my company.

title: Configuration inheritance and override
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want configuration to inherit from platform defaults and be overridable at company and department levels, so that we only override what we need.
acceptance_criteria: Given platform defaults, when a company is configured, then company settings inherit platform defaults and can override them.; Given company settings, when department settings are configured, then departments inherit company settings and can override them where applicable.; Given no override is set at a level, when a setting is resolved, then the inherited value from the parent level applies.; Given an override is set, when the setting is resolved, then the override takes precedence for that scope.

title: Configuration change application semantics
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want configuration changes to apply consistently and be auditable, so that changes are predictable in Phase I.
acceptance_criteria: Given a configuration change, when I save it, then it is logged in the audit trail.; Given a configuration change, when saved, then it is effective immediately with no future-dating in Phase I.; Given a configuration change, when applied, then it affects only new transactions and is not applied retroactively.; Given a prior configuration, when a change is made, then existing and past transactions retain the configuration that was in effect when they occurred.

title: Company branding configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure my company's branding, so that our logo and color appear in the product.
acceptance_criteria: Given company branding, when I configure it, then I can upload a company logo (optional).; Given branding, when I set a primary color, then it must be a valid hex color code in #RRGGBB format.; Given a logo is configured, when the company context is displayed, then the logo can be shown, for example in the context indicator.; Given branding fields are optional, when omitted, then the company still saves without branding.

title: Platform company list view with filters and pagination
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want a company list view with filters, search, and pagination, so that I can browse and manage all tenant companies.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Companies list, when it loads, then it shows columns Code (clickable to detail), Legal Name, Jurisdiction (primary), Status, and Actions.; Given the Status column, when rendered, then it uses color indicators (Green=Active, Yellow=Suspended, Gray=Inactive).; Given the Actions column, when rendered, then it shows contextual actions (View, Edit, Suspend/Activate) based on the company status.; Given filters, when I use them, then I can filter by Status, by Jurisdiction, and by search on name or code.; Given many companies, when I page through results, then pagination is provided with a default page size of 20 and a maximum of 100.; Given a Create Company button, when displayed, then it is visible only to Platform Admin roles.

title: RBAC for company management actions
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want company-management permissions enforced by role, so that only authorized roles perform each action.
acceptance_criteria: Given Create Company, View All Companies, and Edit Any Company, when checked, then they are permitted only for Platform Admin (Platform Super Administrator and Platform Operations Admin).; Given Suspend/Activate Company and Delete Company, when checked, then they are permitted only for the Platform Super Administrator and not the Platform Operations Admin.; Given View Assigned Companies, when checked, then Portfolio Admin sees portfolio companies, Group Company Admin sees group companies, and Company Admin sees only their own company.; Given Edit Assigned Company, when checked, then Portfolio Admin and Group Company Admin have limited edit and Company Admin can edit their own company.; Given an unauthorized role, when it attempts a restricted action, then the action is denied.

title: Tenant isolation enforcement via context and session binding
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want tenant isolation enforced through query scoping and session binding, so that cross-tenant data leakage is impossible.
acceptance_criteria: Given any data query, when executed, then it must include a company_id filter based on the user's current company context.; Given cross-company access, when attempted, then it is permitted only through explicit portfolio or group-company assignments.; Given any cross-company access attempt, when it occurs, then it is logged whether successful or failed.; Given a JWT token, when issued, then it includes the company context and API requests validate that the request matches the token's company claim.; Given an unauthorized company access attempt, when detected, then it is rejected with AUTH_001.

title: Company management audit events
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want company lifecycle and data-access events audited, so that we have a complete compliance trail.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company is created, when it completes, then a COMPANY_CREATED event captures all company data and the creator.; Given company details are modified, when saved, then a COMPANY_UPDATED event captures the changed fields with old and new values, the changed-by user, and the timestamp.; Given a lifecycle transition, when it occurs, then a COMPANY_STATUS_CHANGED event captures old status, new status, and reason.; Given company data is viewed, when accessed, then a COMPANY_ACCESSED event captures the viewer, timestamp, and records accessed.; Given audit events, when stored, then they are retained for 7 years.

title: Standardized company error codes and responses
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want company operations to return standardized error codes, so that failures are consistent and actionable.
acceptance_criteria: Given a duplicate company name, when creating, then COMP_001 (409 Conflict) is returned.; Given an invalid jurisdiction, when submitted, then COMP_002 (400 Bad Request) is returned.; Given a request for a non-existent company, when made, then COMP_003 (404 Not Found) is returned.; Given a suspended company, when an operation is attempted, then COMP_004 (403 Forbidden) is returned.; Given an inactive company, when a modification is attempted, then COMP_005 (409 Conflict) is returned.; Given a duplicate registration number, when saving, then COMP_006 (409 Conflict) is returned.; Given unauthorized company access, when attempted, then AUTH_001 (403 Forbidden) is returned.
